verdict:
2026-07-02 09:06:59 PDT

The approach is sound. The reworked `/close` step now has a coherent declarative record set: `CHANGELOG.md` is gated by root file presence, `BACKLOG.md` is gated by a tracked item, and the procedure explicitly commits only when at least one record edit exists. The zero-record path is first-class enough: it says to make no record commit and proceed on the already-gated HEAD, so the remaining 5(c)/5(d) “record commit/head” wording is slightly imprecise but not a merge-shape footgun.

`BACKLOG.md` and `.claude/workflow-protocol.md` agree with the doctrine: no live doc outside `reviews/` still claims `/close` always writes a changelog, and both preserve “records ride with the merge” while allowing no record commit. `CHANGELOG.md` is deleted in `main...HEAD`; `AUDIT-` remains in the `/close` tracked-backlog wording, so the linter anchor is preserved. No new config/dependency surface was introduced, which matches the DRY/no-third-drifting-record values.
